Title: Elevating seed oil content in a polyploid crop by induced mutations in SEED FATTY ACID REDUCER genes

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GDSL lipase; SGNH hydrolase superfamily; SEED FATTY ACID REDUCER
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant energy reserve; edible vegetable oil yield
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in early seed development (around 25 and 35 DAP), then decreases toward ripening
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Association analysis; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; EMS mutagenesis; CRISPR target attempt; seed oil phenotyping
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Resequencing of 870 accessions; RNA-seq in ZY511 at 16 and 40 DAP; accession NA
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Natural variation in BnSFAR1 is associated with seed oil content and oleic acid variation, but induced single or double mutant combinations tested here did not significantly increase seed oil content
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Nonsynonymous SNPs in BnSFAR1 significantly affected seed oil content and oleic acid content in the association panel. 2. BnSFAR1 expression is strongest at early seed development and declines later. 3. EMS-derived BnSFAR1 double mutants did not show significant seed oil increase. 4. Lack of phenotype may reflect low late-stage expression, redundancy, or possible neofunctionalization.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No beneficial SOC effect established from induced mutants
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Lower priority target than BnSFAR4/5 for oil improvement based on this study
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CRISPR editing attempt for BnSFAR1 failed to recover mutations in T1 plants; despite association signal, functional validation for SOC increase was negative in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GDSL lipase; SGNH hydrolase superfamily; SEED FATTY ACID REDUCER
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Storage triacylglycerol turnover during seed maturation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyses storage triacylglycerols in seeds, releasing fatty acids and glycerol during seed maturation/oil turnover
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with target-product accumulation by degrading stored oil
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for seed oil loss during maturation; supported as major determinant by knockout phenotypes
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant energy reserve; edible vegetable oil yield
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed oil bodies in cotyledon cells
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in developing seeds; subfamily-a high at early seed development (15–35 DAP), subfamily-b high at maturation/desiccation (45–55 DAP)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing seeds, especially 35–55 DAP depending on paralog/subfamily
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed developmental stage; mild salt stress (50 mM NaCl) for vigor assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate gene association analysis; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; EMS mutagenesis; CRISPR-Cas knockout; genotyping; seed oil quantification; fatty acid profiling; TEM oil body analysis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Resequencing of 870 accessions (Wu et al., 2019); RNA-seq of developing seeds at 16 and 40 DAP in cultivar ZY511; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout of multiple BnSFAR4 paralogs significantly increases seed oil content and enlarges oil bodies without reducing germination or seedling vigor
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Nonsynonymous SNPs in BnSFAR4 were significantly associated with higher seed oil content and altered oleic acid content. 2. BnSFAR4 paralogs are expressed in developing seeds, with temporal divergence between subfamily-a and subfamily-b. 3. CRISPR quadruple knockout and EMS double-mutant combinations increased seed oil content by about 8.7–14.5% relative to controls. 4. Mutants showed slower oil decline during seed maturation and larger oil bodies, indicating reduced TAG degradation. 5. No adverse effects on germination, root/shoot growth, or overall plant performance were detected.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious pleiotropic effects on growth, flowering, seed set, germination, vigor, or oil mobilization; slight fatty acid composition shifts in some lines
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for increasing rapeseed oil yield; multiple-paralog knockout in elite cultivars is recommended; CRISPR is most efficient where permitted, EMS useful in GMO-restricted jurisdictions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Four paralogs were identified and divided into subfamily-a and subfamily-b; additive action inferred because single mutants did not increase oil content while multiple knockouts did.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GDSL lipase; SGNH hydrolase superfamily; SEED FATTY ACID REDUCER
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Storage triacylglycerol turnover during seed maturation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyses storage triacylglycerols in seeds, reducing final retained seed oil
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with target-product accumulation by degrading stored oil
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point supported by double-knockout phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant energy reserve; edible vegetable oil yield
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in developing seeds; one paralog showed cultivar-specific expression difference noted in discussion
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Association analysis for fatty acid composition; RT-qPCR/RNA-seq expression profiling; CRISPR-Cas mutagenesis; seed oil phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Resequencing of 870 accessions; RNA-seq of developing seeds at 16 and 40 DAP in ZY511; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Double knockout of BnSFAR5 significantly increases seed oil content without obvious growth or vigor penalty
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SNPs in BnSFAR5 significantly affected oleic acid content. 2. CRISPR-generated double mutants in both BnSFAR5 paralogs increased seed oil content by about 10.4–11.2%. 3. Mutants showed normal thousand-kernel weight and plant performance. 4. The results support BnSFAR5 as a negative regulator of oil retention via lipid degradation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant penalty in TKW or overall plant performance reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to elevate oil yield, alone or stacked with BnSFAR4 knockouts in elite backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Two paralogs were edited; T1 plants were chimeric, but homozygous double mutants were recovered by T3.
